idea-plugin.name: Lazygit
idea-plugin.description:
Bring the speed and elegance of Lazygit directly into your JetBrains IDE. No more context switching between your terminal and your editor.

✨ Zero-Config Native File Editing

Forget about writing custom bash scripts or fighting with complex $EDITOR configurations. This plugin features a custom, high-performance IPC (Inter-Process Communication) bridge. Simply press e on any file in Lazygit, and it will instantly open right in your IDE's editor. It works completely out of the box in any IntelliJ-based IDE without a single line of extra configuration.

🚀 Key Features

  • Immersive UI: Toggles Lazygit in a dedicated, full-screen editor tab rather than a cramped tool window, giving your git graph the space it deserves.
  • Instant Edit: Native file opening via IPC. Hit e and start typing.
  • Smart Lifecycle: Automatically cleans up and closes the IDE tab the moment you exit Lazygit (pressing q).
  • IdeaVim Friendly: Easily map the toggle action (:action Lazygit.Toggle) to your favorite Vim keybinding for a seamless workflow.

Note: You must have the Lazygit executable installed on your system to use this plugin.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
Version (0.1.2):
  • Feature: Added support for opening multiple selected files at once (using visual/select mode and pressing 'e').

Version (0.1.1):
  • Fix: Correctly isolate lazygit instances per project window to fix file opening in multiple IDE instances.

Initial release (0.1.0):
  • Toggle LazyGit in a full-screen editor tab.
  • Native file opening (press 'e' in LazyGit to open in IDE).
  • Automatic closing of editor tab when exiting LazyGit.
  • IdeaVim support via :action Lazygit.Toggle.
